resources.string: The receive link test bench in developer mode only retains the input, observation and diagnostic capabilities required for current troubleshooting of RX.

The current goal is not to continue fine-tuning WPM, but to get a fresh look at how the RX frontend actually behaves on real devices. Use this page to focus on the input source, spectrum, lock status, threshold, timing and decoding output before deciding on the next round of architectural adjustments.
The experimental bench only serves development and verification. The normal user path should not bear the burden of these experimental controls and historical debugging.
Runtime permissions are sufficient for current debugging needs
Audio frame callback: %1$d times\nAccumulated sampling points: %2$d\nCurrent source status: %3$s
Recent events: tone on/off events have not been generated yet
Latest timing event: not yet generated
Recent timing events: %1$s / %2$s\nDuration: %3$d ms\nDot Estimate: %4$d ms
Recent decoding events: not yet generated
RAW: No original character stream yet
Candidate call signs: No candidates yet
Recent interpretation events: not yet generated
This input source is still in the occupying stage. Currently, only the mobile phone microphone is connected.
There are %1$d templates in total, and the Operate page will dynamically read the list here.
Are you sure you want to delete template "%1$s"?
%1$d templates have been imported, %2$d have been added.
%1$d templates have been imported, %2$d have been added, of which %3$d have been automatically renamed due to duplicate names.
%1$d templates have been imported, %2$d have been updated, and %3$d have been added.
%1$d templates have been imported, %2$d have been updated, %3$d have been added, and %4$d names have been automatically adjusted.
There are no importable templates in this file.
%1$d templates detected. Please select the import method:
After creating a new one, it will be added to the sending template list.
The new template will appear in the template list on the Operate page immediately after saving.
After modification, save it and the Operate page will read the latest text here.
During editing, it will be synchronized to the Operate template selector after saving.
The new template editing content has been cleared.
The original content of the template has been restored.
For example: CQ, short response, closing
For example: CQ CQ DE <MYCALL> K
Choose whether CWCN follows the system language or uses a fixed app language.
There are no QSO records to share.
LAN export has not started yet.
Open the following address in the same LAN browser to download ADI:\n\n%1$s
The export address has been copied.
There are currently no QSO records available for export.
Are you sure you want to delete the record for %1$s?
This QSO record cannot be deleted.
Create a new official CW contact record
Call sign of the other party
The original record no longer exists.
Cleared to a new blank record.
Current draft seed has been restored.
The current radio frequency has been brought in.
A draft seed has been brought in.
Local input is saved internally as UTC and is also written as UTC when exporting ADIF.
The other party\'s call sign cannot be empty.
The communication time must comply with yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.
There is currently no draft seed. You can create a new manual QSO record here.
The initial values ​​have been brought in from the current RX/TX context, and the call sign and time can still be adjusted before saving.
Please enter local time. CWCN automatically saves UTC and exports ADIF as UTC.
UTC export: %1$s | Local display can be modified directly.
Type or apply a template, then send
TX path selected, but rig RX audio is not connected yet.
Tap here to grant microphone access and start phone RX.
Currently working in external audio input mode, please confirm that the USB audio device is connected and routed to the recording input by Android.
The current combination is: RX mobile phone microphone / TX independent line control / CAT USB serial port.
The current main interface only retains transcript and RAW transcripts, and log judgment is done manually.
Pausing, the current character will be resent
Paused, right click to continue the current character
Candidate call sign: Waiting for clearer RX turn

There are currently no new RX candidates. 

Selected call sign: %1$s

If you need to rewrite the log, please press and hold; if you need to cancel manual selection, you can clear it here.
There are currently no callsign candidates available.
There are currently no call sign candidates available.
The log repository is currently unavailable.
The current call sign is invalid.
Select candidate call signs and write to log
Select the current call sign of the other party
Based on the current RAW candidate, enter log editing after selection.
Based on the current RAW candidate, the call sign will be used as template and log seed when selected.
The manually selected call sign of the other party has been cleared.
No transmit link available in setup
There are currently no reserved RX/TX entries
New transceiver turns will continue to be stacked here in chronological order
Microphone permission needs to be granted before CWCN can receive via the phone microphone.
Currently listening to incoming CW through the phone microphone.
There is currently no active receive link, please check the settings or radio configuration.
The mobile phone input has been clipped, so first move the microphone farther away or lower the monitoring volume, otherwise the WPM will be pushed up and the decoding will be overwhelmed.
The input of the mobile phone is too hot. It is recommended to zoom out a little further to see if the decoding is stable again.
Adjust reception threshold and transmission speed
There are currently no active receive sessions. \n\nAfter enabling reception, the current receiving snapshot will be displayed here.
There are currently no active receive sessions.
Reserve space for weak CW to cross the line
Use this set of values as the current RX/TX tuning reference.
MAN is the manual threshold, IN is the current input, and REC is the recommended threshold.
MAN is just above IN. This is a good spot to wait for valid CW to cross the line.%1$s
MAN is %1$d above the current input, so background noise should stay below it.%2$s
Tone is %1$d above MAN, so valid CW should be able to pass.%2$s
Noise is near or above MAN. Consider raising the threshold.%1$s
IN is %1$d above MAN. If this is not CW, consider raising the threshold.%2$s
, based on a threshold slightly above the estimated noise.
USB permission request has been sent, please allow it in the system pop-up window.
Unable to initiate USB permission request at this time: %1$s

This section stores your station profile for normal operation. Callsign, name, QTH, Maidenhead grid, and template variables such as <RIG> / <ANT> / <WX> are all sourced from here, so there is no separate save action. The Maidenhead grid can be filled automatically from the device\'s most recent location and converted to a 4-character grid.

View the current official routing here, the real radio station selection and detection is still completed in "Radio Configuration". When the official external audio input is unavailable, whether to fall back to the mobile phone microphone and mobile phone audio is determined by "No Radio Coverage".
In Auto mode, RX can fall back to the phone microphone and TX can fall back to phone audio whenever the primary rig RX/TX path is unavailable. Rig-only mode disables this fallback entirely.
Only the CAT/Keying summary of the current official station path is shown here. Connection parameter modification, USB permissions and PTT/keying verification are unified in "Radio Configuration".
Please open "Radio Configuration" first, select and fix an official radio path.
Connection parameter modification, USB permission application and PTT/keying joint debugging have been unified into "Radio Configuration". Only the current official path summary is shown here.

There are three groups of configurations: transmit default value, receive tone strategy, and receive input source. The default WPM and default tone are used to transmit the starting value; the tone tracking determines whether to prefer a fixed tone or subsequent automatic tracking when receiving; the frequency locking window determines the width of the search around the default tone. Under normal circumstances, it is recommended to maintain "Standard ±50Hz"; when the sample is very dirty and the center frequency is known, you can try "Tight Lock ±30Hz"; if you are worried about being too narrow, you can also switch to "Loose ±70Hz" or directly customize the value.

This opens the dedicated TX template list. Templates can be added, edited, and deleted here, and Operate reads the latest content dynamically.

Supported variables: <MYCALL>, <CALL>, <RST>, <RST_SENT>, <RST_RCVD>, <NAME>, <QTH>, <GRID>, <RIG>, <ANT>, <WX>.

<RST> and <RST_SENT> are equivalent and represent the report you send to the other station. <RST_RCVD> is the report received from the other station.
Development tools remain hidden by default. Temporary display is only recommended during RX, TX or link joint debugging.
RX observation / TX joint debugging / radio diagnosis
Development tools are hidden by default
Location permission is required to auto-fill the grid.
Positioning permission is required to get the grid automatically.
The system has no location services available.
There is no nearest location available and automatic grid retrieval is not possible at this time.
No nearest location is available yet.
The nearest location cannot be converted to a valid grid.
The current location cannot be converted to a Maidenhead grid.
Grid %1$s has been auto-filled based on recent locations.
The station data has been cleared and is falling back to draft/log inference.
Station data has been automatically saved.
During editing, it will automatically save after leaving the input box.
Sending templates have been migrated to independent list page management.
The joint calling tool is currently displayed.
It is currently the official view and the joint calling tool has been hidden.
Some station data, missing items will continue to fall back
No official station information has been saved yet
No official route is currently fixed
CW parameters have been automatically saved to %1$s.
There is currently no fixed official route, please go to "Radio Configuration" to select it first.
Link parameters have been automatically saved to %1$s.
No radio stations have been automatically saved as: %1$s.
Automatic: Cell Phone Microphone / Cell Phone Audio
When the official radio transceiver link is unavailable, reception falls back to the phone\'s microphone and transmission falls back to the phone\'s audio.
When the official radio transceiver link is unavailable, the mobile phone back-up link is not enabled.
When there is an official external audio input, try the external audio first; when it is unavailable, follow the back-up strategy and return to the mobile phone microphone.
Always use your phone\'s microphone as the receiving input.
Prefer Android routed USB external audio input.
Priority is given to bypassing system voice enhancement, which is suitable for fixed sidetone testing.
Some mobile phones will turn off part of the call processing, which is suitable as a compromise mode.
The highest compatibility, but the most likely to bring into the system noise reduction and voice processing.
Make a narrow window lock around the default tone to prioritize reducing false tracking.
The opening remains conservatively locked, and the follow-up allows for automatic tracking, better suited for unknown tones.
Next step: First enter "Radio Configuration" to fix an official route; if there is no radio station for the time being, you can keep your mobile phone for backup.
Next step: The current USB keying adapter has not been connected yet, please go back to "Radio Configuration" to check the fixed route.
Next: USB keying is ready. Return to Operate for live TX testing.
Next step: Plug in the OTG/USB control device and come back here to request permissions.
Next step: First click "Apply for USB Permission" to confirm that the Android authorization is passed.
Next step: Complete the serial CAT port, or fill in the independent keying port, and then save the settings.
Next step: The serial CAT basic link is now available, and PTT/keying joint debugging can be started.
Next step: Supplement the CI-V address and continue serial port CAT joint debugging.
Next step: Check whether the serial port permissions, baud rate and port prompts are consistent with the actual radio.
Next step: First fill in the CAT port of Xiegu; if the site is also going to use an independent keying port, then fill in the keying port and save it.
Next step: Supplement the Xiegu device address, and then continue serial port CAT joint debugging.
Next step: Check whether Xiegu’s serial port permissions, baud rate, device address and port prompts are consistent with the current radio.

Next step: Xiegu serial port CAT is ready, you can continue to verify the frequency reading, and decide whether RX uses USB audio or mobile phone microphone hybrid mode according to on-site conditions.
Next step: Xiegu serial port CAT is ready, you can continue to verify the frequency reading, and connect the RX / TX audio link of the G90 series before joint debugging.
Next step: Fill in the rigctld host and port, and then save the settings.
Next: Network CAT basic setup is complete. You can now verify rigctld connectivity and TX control.
Next step: Confirm that rigctld has been started on the target host and the mobile phone is currently reachable by the network.
Next: Connect the audio cable, calibrate the VOX threshold, then return to Operate to verify short-message TX.
Next step: Complete the basic configuration of the current route first, and then go back to Operate to verify the official link.
WPM is recommended to stay between 5 and 80.
The pitch is recommended to stay between 200 and 2000 Hz.
The frequency lock window must be an integer.
It is recommended that the frequency locking window be kept between %1$d and %2$d Hz.
The baud rate must be an integer.
The baud rate is recommended to be between 300 and 921600.
It is recommended to keep the port between 1 and 65535.
Currently the fixed route is not connected to the USB keying adapter.
USB permission request has been sent, please allow it in the Android pop-up window before returning here.
A USB permission request cannot be initiated at this time. Status: %1$s
CI-V addresses must be 1 to 2 hexadecimal digits.
This is used for joint debugging of transmission links, including plan preview, template filling, transmission path and transmission progress observation.
Record USB permissions, link changes, diagnostic stages and launch results by time to facilitate joint debugging and review.
Unable to switch USB keying line while TX is running.
There is nothing to send yet.
TX startup failed. \nReason: The backend rejected this request. \nNext step: %1$s

Tempo: %1$d WPM
Tone: %2$d Hz
Dot: %3$d ms
Number of elements: %4$d
Estimated duration: %5$s

Transmit path: %1$s
Link description: %2$s
Ready: %3$s
Current status: %4$s
Using WPM: %5$s
Using tone frequency: %6$s
Supports real-time configuration: %7$s
Supports progress snapshot: %8$s
\nTX Note: The joint debugging station is currently temporarily using a transmitting backend that is different from the fixed radio path.
\nTX Note: The current fixed radio path here does not expose a text-to-CW backend that can be used directly.

TX is idle. Independent keying links are on standby. \nPrepare a short plan first, then click "Start TX". \nThis path will not generate cell phone sidetone, please rely on the radio\'s own monitoring or sidetone.
TX is idle. \nAfter preparing the launch plan, click "Start TX".

TX is idle. 
The current launch path is not ready yet. 
Reason: %1$s
Next step: %2$s

Target mode: %1$s
Target status: %2$s
Diagnostic phase: %3$s (%4$s)
Current target: %5$s
Availability: %6$s
Next step: %7$s
Candidate device detected, but current target has not yet been determined
Please select a TX path first.
Just click "Start TX" to do an offline practice; if the sound is too loud, please lower the volume of your mobile phone or wear headphones.
First connect the audio link and turn on VOX, then start testing with a conservative volume and short template.

First fix the Yaesu serial port CAT configuration, confirm the detected serial port and baud rate in the radio configuration, and verify that the CAT detection is successful, then retry a short VVV or DIT transmission.

First fix the Xiegu serial port CAT configuration, and confirm the serial port, baud rate and necessary CI-V style parameters in the radio configuration; if the RX audio has not yet been connected, you can first switch to "Hybrid Mode: RX Mobile Microphone" and then retry a short transmission.
First fix the Icom CI-V configuration, confirm the serial port, baud rate and CI-V address in the radio configuration, and verify that the detection is successful, then retry a short transmission.
First fix the Kenwood serial port CAT configuration, confirm that the serial port link is ready in the radio configuration and verify the detection response, and then retry a short transmission.
First open the radio configuration, fix the matching serial port CAT configuration, confirm that the detection is ready, and then retry a short independent keyed CW transmission.
First fix the rigctld configuration of the Yaesu FT family, confirm that the host and port are reachable, and verify that rigctld is bound to the radio, then retry a short DIT or VVV send_morse test.

After first fixing the Icom family\'s rigctld configuration, confirming that the host and port are reachable, and verifying that rigctld is bound to the CI-V radio, retry a short DIT or VVV send_morse test.

After first fixing the Kenwood family\'s rigctld configuration, confirming that the host and port are reachable, and verifying that rigctld is bound to the radio, retry a short DIT or VVV send_morse test.
First fix a Hamlib rigctld network configuration in the radio configuration, confirm that the host and port are reachable, and then retry the send_morse test with a short CQ or DIT length.
Please check the current link description and availability status before trying again.
Reconnect the locked USB keyed device, or switch the device selection back to automatic and click "Refresh USB Device".
Plug in a CDC/ACM USB device and click "Refresh USB Device".
Plug in a CDC/ACM compliant USB serial or keyed device and refresh the current link.
This USB device does not expose the expected CDC control interface. Please try another keying configuration or device.
Refresh the USB device first, re-plug the cable or OTG adapter, and then retry the short DIT test.
First release the keying wire, refresh the USB link, and confirm that no other application is occupying this interface.
Please select a connected target device, or switch back to automatic selection.
Click "Apply for USB Permission" first. If you suspect that the control line is stuck, perform "Release Keying Line" after permission is passed.
Load the DIT test or VVV test first, and then click "Start TX" after confirming that the keying line wiring is correct.
The USB serial transmit backend is currently not selected.
The USB backend has permissions and is ready.
The simulated USB permissions process is completed immediately.
USB permission request issued. If the device pops up, please allow it in the system.
USB device inventory and link status refreshed.
The USB keying line has been released and the link state has been reset.
There are currently no USB keying wires to release, but the link status has still been refreshed.
A short USB authentication template has been loaded. Please confirm the wiring before starting TX.
USB permission granted, rechecking backend readiness status.
The currently selected USB keying device has been disconnected: %1$s
Local sidetone (station path not fixed yet)
The clipboard is not available on the current device.
The USB serial link is currently not selected.
The TX joint debugging station has been opened.
The device selector has been switched back to auto-targeting mode.
The emulated USB scenario has been switched to %1$s.
Launch intercepted: The current launch plan is empty.
Start blocked: %1$s is currently unavailable, reason: %2$s
Launch Ignored: The current launch path is already running.
Requested to start emission on %1$s of %2$d elements.
The stop button was pressed, but %1$s is currently idle.
USB permission request ignored: The USB serial transmit backend is not currently selected.
USB permission request skipped: current backend is ready.
A USB permission request has been made to the current target device.
Refresh ignored: The USB serial transmit backend is not currently selected.
Manual refresh of USB link status requested.
Release key wire ignored: The USB serial transmit backend is not currently selected.
The keying wire has been released and the USB link status has been refreshed.
Release requested, but no keyed line is currently active; USB link status has still been refreshed.
Validation template ignored: USB serial transmit backend is not currently selected.
System authorization pop-up window has granted USB permission.
The system authorization pop-up has denied USB permission.
The locked target device is currently unavailable: %1$s.
The current TX transmit verification report has been copied to the clipboard.
RX continues to be determined by the input strategy of "Settings > CW Parameters"; TX and CAT are executed according to the current radio path.
Force RX to follow the cell phone microphone, and TX and CAT to continue following the current radio path. Suitable for USB models such as FT-891 that only support CAT/wire control.
CW speed and pitch are maintained uniformly in "Settings > CW Parameters", where only the connection parameters of the current radio path are retained.
Request serial port CAT USB permission
Run full scan of key lines
The speed and pitch of the audio VOX are also taken from "Settings > CW Parameters", and the delay and sensitivity of the VOX still need to be calibrated on the radio side.
%1$s has been set as the current official station path. \nSending and receiving combination: %2$s
Recommended parameters for %1$s have been restored.
Preparing USB serial port CAT permission request...
The matched device already has USB serial port CAT permission.
USB serial port CAT permission request issued. If Android prompts for system authorization, please allow it and then try the detection again.
A USB serial port CAT permission request cannot be initiated at this time. USB status: %1$s
USB serial port CAT permission process failed before Android pop-up window: %1$s
USB serial port CAT permission process abnormally interrupted: %1$s
Serial CAT probe aborted before completion: %1$s
CAT transmit pulse verification exited before completion: %1$s
Running Independent Keyed Port Pulse Verification...
Independent keyed port pulse verification exited abnormally before completion: %1$s
Running independent key hold verification. Please observe whether TX continues to be valid during the 1.5 second hold period.
Independent key hold verification exited before completion: %1$s
Keyport switch verification is running. Do not actively switch DTR/RTS, please observe whether abnormal TX flashing occurs when turning on or off.
Keyport switch verification exited abnormally before completion: %1$s
Running DTR timing verification. Look at TX, RF power, and sidetone simultaneously; this path normalizes the control line first, then pulls high, holds, and releases in sequence.
DTR timing verification exited before completion: %1$s
Running short pulse verification. Pay close attention to edge behavior: TX latching, RF output, sidetone, and whether short elements fall back to RX.
Short pulse verification exited before completion: %1$s
Scanning all detected serial ports, control lines, and polarity combinations. Watch the station and note the first step that actually triggers the TX.
Keyed line scan failed to start: No USB serial port detected for testing.
Keyed line scan step %1$d/%2$d: %3$s. Please watch the radio reaction now.
Key line scan completed. If the rig responded, note the matching step below:\n%1$s
It will detect whether the host and port are reachable, and request radio information from rigctld without starting TX.
You can use "Verify rigctld connection" to confirm whether the current host and port are reachable.
Currently connection detection is only provided when the network CAT selection is Hamlib rigctld.
The USB port for serial CAT has not been detected yet.
%1$d serial CAT ports have been detected. Please select the one you want to test in the port selector first.

%1$s Yaesu currently recommends using a mixed link: CAT uses a USB serial port, TX uses an independent wire control, and RX uses a mobile phone microphone or an independent USB audio interface. For models like the FT-891, do not use the body USB as an audio input.

%1$s For Xiegu X6100 / X6200, the current recommendation is a single-USB setup: CAT over the built-in USB serial port, RX preferably over USB external audio, and Hybrid Mode: RX Phone Microphone only when field conditions require it.

%1$s The Xiegu G90 series is currently processed according to the on-site form of "separation of CAT and audio": first ensure that the serial port CAT is normal, and RX can use the mobile phone microphone first, or connect to an external audio interface later.
%1$s now only retains the minimum handshake check. More in-depth control line experiments have been moved to the development tools portal.
%1$s Currently, serial port CAT connection verification has been prioritized for Yaesu style, Icom CI-V and Kenwood style.

%1$s Yaesu Note: Old CAT TX/PTT pulses are still covering the early TX1/TX0 paths; if you are verifying DTR/RTS, please use independent keyed port pulses in preference. If the problem focuses on short E/T/EEE/VVV behavior, please give priority to short pulse experiments rather than long hold verification. The current experience of FT-710 is: when the radio menu is set to PC KEYING = DTR, RTS + DTR is still the currently most accepted combination; only RTS may have TX/RF but sidetone is abnormal, and only DTR is currently still considered unavailable.

%1$s Xiegu serial port CAT has been connected to the current detection skeleton. In the next step, fill in the device address first, and then do safe frequency reading or model query; if the RX audio is not connected temporarily, you can use "Hybrid Mode: RX Mobile Microphone" to complete the first round of joint debugging.

%1$s For Xiegu X6100 / X6200, verify the device address, frequency readback, and USB serial link first. RX should preferably use USB external audio, with Hybrid Mode: RX Phone Microphone as a fallback when needed.

%1$s Xiegu G90 series currently recommends completing device address and frequency reading verification first; it is more like the on-site form of "separating CAT and audio", and then connecting RX / TX audio separately.
%1$s Icom CI-V is detectable. First set the CI-V address, then initiate a secure transceiver ID query, and then use short CI-V PTT pulses for the next step of smoke verification.
%1$s Icom CI-V is detectable. After confirming that the transceiver ID query is normal, use short CI-V PTT pulses for the next step of smoke verification.
%1$s Kenwood style CAT is detectable. It is recommended to perform secure ASCII read operations such as ID;/FA;/IF; before entering the TX side for verification.
%1$s Serial CAT detection currently preferentially connects to Yaesu style, Icom CI-V and Kenwood style.
Extended probing, control line experiments, and timing verification are all concentrated here to avoid polluting the formal configuration path.
Select the official radio station path and save the connection parameters that are really needed for this path.
1. First select the radio station path you plan to use. \n2. Set it to the current official path. \n3. Only fill in the connection parameters that are really needed for this path.
\nNext step: Confirm the baud rate first
, then select the CAT port; if the radio also has an independent keying port, add the keying line and keying port, and finally save this path.

\nRecommended combination: RX uses a mobile phone microphone or independent USB audio interface, TX uses an independent wire control, and CAT uses the body USB serial port. On Yaesu models like the FT-891, the USB on the body is not responsible for receiving audio input.

\nRecommended combination: CAT uses the body\'s USB serial port, and RX gives priority to the USB external audio; if the on-site USB audio conditions are not ideal, you can temporarily switch to "Hybrid Mode: RX Mobile Microphone".

\nRecommended combination: first fix CAT to the serial port link, and RX to use the mobile phone microphone or external audio interface; if formal CW transmission is to be done later, add an independent keying port or a complete audio link.
\nThe mixed mode is currently enabled: RX uses the mobile phone microphone, TX continues to use the current line control, and CAT continues to use the current USB serial port.
\nDeeper protocol verification will remain hidden in normal mode.
\nMore in-depth protocol verification has been included in the development tool portal.
\nNext step: Fill in the host and port of the radio bridge, and then save this path.
\nConnection detection will remain hidden in normal mode.
\nConnection detection is now available in Developer Tools.
\nNext step: Select RTS or DTR and lock the preferred USB device if necessary; if Android pops up a permission request, allow it before saving.
\nNext step: Save this audio path, and then continue to fine-tune the VOX delay and sensitivity on the radio side.
\nNext step: Save the parameters of this path first, and then return to the corresponding operation page to continue verification.
There is currently no official station path. \nSelect the one you use most first, and the subsequent RX/TX pages will follow by default.
\nThe current combination changes have not been applied yet. Operate will switch to the new mode only after clicking "Apply current path combination".
The recommended parameters that come with this path
\nThe current official path: this is it
\nCurrent official path: %1$s (If you need to switch, click "Set as current path")
First select a radio path and then fill in its own connection parameters.
Using the parameters you saved for this path.
Currently these are still the recommended parameters that come with this path.

Available transport layers: %1$d/%2$d
Current verifiable paths: %3$d/%4$d
Current strategy: First stabilize the reusable family paths, and then gradually add specific models. 
Development experimental capabilities continue to be retained, but they are auxiliary means and are no longer mixed with the formal configuration path.
\n1. If you are currently connecting to the Yaesu FT series, start from %1$s first, so that while the native serial port CAT continues to close, CWCN can still reuse the existing rigctld TX path.
\n2. The Icom family currently prefers %1$s as the first official path.
\n3. The Kenwood family currently prefers %1$s as the first official path.
\n4. Make %1$s stable as the first truly implementable wired control path.
\n5. Reserve %1$s as a compatible fallback path.
\n6. Keep the reusable CAT structure stable before continuing to integrate deeper Yaesu/Icom/Kenwood native model logic.
\n7. The radio configuration page already has Yaesu style, Icom CI-V and Kenwood style native serial port CAT basic detection capabilities.
\n8. Development experiment capabilities continue to be retained, but they should be viewed as secondary engineering paths rather than the main user flow.
\n9. The next step of native work is no longer to configure the skeleton, but to continue to hook up controllable family-level serial port CAT behavior based on shared detection and session seams.
USB keying permission request issued. If Android pops up for system authorization, please allow it before returning here.
A USB keying permission request cannot be initiated at this time. Status: %1$s
USB keying permission request failed before Android popup: %1$s
USB keying permission process aborted abnormally: %1$s
Performing USB keyed line pulse test...
Requested to stop current USB keying send...
The stop request does not change the current USB keying state.
USB key line pulse cannot be started. Current status: %1$s
USB key line pulsing completed. Please observe whether the radio station actually turns on briefly.
The USB keying cable was pulled up but failed to release. Please check this USB path before transmitting.
USB key line pulse aborted before completion: %1$s
This USB keying path does not support text-to-CW sending.
USB test text "%1$s" completed. Please observe whether the station transmits the entire paragraph completely.
USB test text "%1$s" was stopped before completion.
USB test text "%1$s" ended with error: %2$s
USB test text "%1$s" failed to complete. Current status: %2$s
USB test text "%1$s" aborted before completion: %2$s
There is currently no USB keying adapter attached to this path.

USB keying path: %1$s
Status: %2$s
Control line: %3$s
Device selection: %4$s
Device lock: %5$s
\nTip: The saved target device is not currently connected.
\nTip: No available USB keying device has been detected.
\nTip: The device has been detected. Next step is to apply for Android USB permission.
\nTip: This USB keying path is ready for launch.
The system has granted USB keying permission.
The system has granted USB serial port CAT permission.
USB serial port CAT permission denied.
. Next step: First select the serial port CAT protocol, and then click "Request Serial Port CAT USB Permission".
The joint debugging entrance is concentrated here to avoid interfering with the official use path.

Developer mode is on. \nCurrently, the RX experimental platform, TX joint debugging platform and radio experimental platform are retained, and other miscellaneous items will no longer enter the official path.
Developer mode is turned off. \nIt is only recommended to temporarily enable it when underlying transceiver troubleshooting, link experiments or protocol verification are required.

The RX test bench focuses on receiving link and decoding issues, on-site trace playback is used to review Operate real machine input, the TX joint debugging bench is used for transmission verification, and the radio test bench is used for CAT/keying experiments.
Development tools will be displayed after turning on developer mode.
Recent scene Trace: None. \nFirst reproduce it once under the Operate real machine path, and the system will automatically record the latest WAV and timeline.
Open the TX joint debugging station
Offline analysis (shared replay): %1$d frames | %2$d tone | %3$d decode | %4$d turns | %5$d repairs | locked %6$s | WPM %7$.1f\n%8$s\nPreview: %9$s
Development tip: startup raw spectrum hint is not implemented.
Development tip: There is no conservative startup fixed Tone suggestion (only based on raw spectrum, not shared replay conclusion; %1$s)
Development tip: You can try to fix Tone %1$d Hz (only based on startup raw spectrum, non-shared replay conclusion; %2$s, %3$d frames)
